title: AI-DLC Settings
description: Configuration schema for .ai-dlc/settings.yml files that configure AI-DLC behavior including VCS strategy

_override_pattern.description: AI-DLC supports a Master + Overrides configuration pattern. Global settings in settings.yml apply to all intents. Per-intent overrides in .ai-dlc/{intent}/settings.yml take precedence. Per-unit overrides in unit frontmatter take highest precedence. Precedence: unit frontmatter > intent settings > global settings.

providers.description: External system providers for spec, ticketing, design, and comms

workflow_mode.description: interactive: pause for user approval at key decision points. autonomous: proceed automatically, only pause on blockers.

granularity.description: coarse: fewer, larger units with minimal process. standard: balanced unit decomposition. fine: many small units with detailed specs.

mockup_format.description: Format for UI mockups generated during elaboration. 'ascii' produces text-based wireframes in markdown code blocks. 'html' produces self-contained HTML files written to .ai-dlc/{intent-slug}/mockups/.

quality_gates.description: Quality gates (backpressure checks) to run during execution. When configured, these replace the default backpressure behavior.

review_agents.description: Configure which specialized review agents run for this project

stack.description: Infrastructure stack configuration describing the deployment and operations layers

model_profiles.description: Map hats to model tiers for cost optimization. Defaults to inheriting the session model for all hats.

planner.description: Model for planning (benefits from deeper reasoning)

ticketing.description: Ticketing/issue tracking provider (Jira, Linear, GitHub Issues, GitLab Issues)

config.description: Provider-specific configuration — schema determined by provider type

instructions.description: Inline provider instructions (merged with built-in defaults and project overrides)

config.description: Provider-specific configuration — schema determined by provider type

instructions.description: Inline provider instructions (merged with built-in defaults and project overrides)

config.description: Provider-specific configuration — schema determined by provider type

instructions.description: Inline provider instructions (merged with built-in defaults and project overrides)

config.description: Provider-specific configuration — schema determined by provider type

instructions.description: Inline provider instructions (merged with built-in defaults and project overrides)

qualityGatesConfig.description: Quality gate configuration for backpressure checks during execution

test.description: Test command to run (e.g., 'bun test', 'npm test', 'pytest')

lint.description: Lint command to run (e.g., 'biome check', 'eslint .', 'ruff check')

manifest_validate.description: Deployment manifest validation command (e.g., 'kubectl --dry-run=client', 'helm template | kubeval')

observable_validate.description: Observability validation command (e.g., verify metric imports, validate dashboard JSON)

operations_validate.description: Operations validation command (e.g., run scripts with --dry-run, validate manifests)

command.description: Shell command to execute for this quality gate

oneOf.title: Review each unit individually
oneOf.description: Each unit opens its own PR/MR. Dependent units wait until their dependencies are merged.

oneOf.title: Build everything, then open one MR
oneOf.description: Units merge into an intent branch as they complete. One final MR for the whole intent.

oneOf.title: Build everything on default branch
oneOf.description: All work happens directly on the default branch. No feature branches or MRs.

elaboration_review.description: Whether to create a pull request for elaborated intent review before planning

default_branch.description: The default/main branch name. Set to 'auto' to detect from remote (origin/HEAD), or specify explicitly (e.g., 'main', 'master', 'develop')

auto_merge.description: Whether to automatically merge completed unit branches into the intent branch. Only applies to 'intent' strategy. For 'unit' strategy, merging is the user's responsibility via PRs.

auto_squash.description: Whether to squash commits when merging. Only applies when auto_merge is enabled.

stackConfig.description: Infrastructure stack configuration. Each layer is optional. An empty stack ({}) is valid.

compute.description: Compute platform providers (Kubernetes, ECS, Lambda, Docker Compose)

pipeline.description: CI/CD pipeline providers (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, CircleCI)

secrets.description: Secrets management providers (Vault, AWS SM, GCP SM, env)

monitoring.description: Monitoring providers (Prometheus, Datadog, CloudWatch, New Relic, OTel)

operations.description: Operations runtime configuration (runtime, scheduled tasks, reactive handlers)
